mentions.note: Oil plays are paradoxical to time on Trump rhetoric: when he says talks are going well, oil weakens (sell); when he warns of escalation, oil rallies (buy). Today's Iran strike cancellation drove oil lower. Focus on fundamentals and domestic supply insulation rather than geopolitical headlines.
Oil price volatile with Iran headlines. Trump's contradictory statements (seize Kharg Island vs. cancel strikes) create reverse-signal dynamics—buy on bad news, sell on good news rhetoric.
Preferred large-cap oil play over BP.
Iran conflict pushing oil prices; 3.8% dividend yield attractive at elevated commodity levels; strong cash flow generation; Mike Wirth running company; pull the trigger on weakness.
Friday report; monitoring for Iran war impact on oil plans; classic long-term approach but oil transport strategy may shift.
Caller Sean asks about offshore drilling supercycle; Cramer says just buy Chevron; CEO Mike Wirth drills offshore best; superior to service drillers.
Worst Dow performer with oil down ~15%; live by oil price, die by oil price in ceasefire-driven reversal.
More forward-looking than Exxon; preferred E&P play for new buyers; Cramer regrets selling his own oil; Chevron is his pick for energy exposure; up 34% YTD.
Oil major rallying on Middle East conflict premium alongside 82nd Airborne deployment; rare day when energy and consumer both gain.
If oil soars to $120+ due to Iran war escalation, even Chevron would get crushed as part of broad S&P 500 selloff. Wait for war resolution before buying energy.
Substantial Berkshire stake; underperforming oil sector rally; market skepticism overriding exposure thesis.
Michael Wirth best-in-class CEO, great assets, solid yield and balance sheet, but parabolic move too high too fast; sell half or quarter position Monday per protocol.

Big buyback, 4% yield, consistent. Possible Venezuela kicker; favorite for long time.
Leading rally today alongside Exxon on oil higher; problematic leadership; when oils win, everybody else losing; energy is tax on economy.
